description: You are a look-development artist creating a weathered vehicle material. Build a procedural rust-over-metal shader for the BMW car body using Blender's Shader Editor. The material must show realistic paint deterioration where oxidation breaks through a metallic surface. Build a node network with: (1) a Noise Texture (Scale 5.0, Detail 8.0) connected to a Color Ramp whose black-to-white threshold is at position 0.4, generating the rust pattern mask; (2) two Principled BSDF shaders -- one for clean metal (dark grey Base Color, Metallic 1.0, Roughness 0.2) and one for rust (brown Base Color, Metallic 0.0, Roughness 0.8); (3) a Mix Shader blending both BSDFs, driven by the Color Ramp output as its Factor; (4) a Voronoi Texture (Scale 15.0) connected through a Bump node (Strength 0.3) to the Normal input of both BSDFs for corrosion pitting detail. Apply this material to the car body object named 'CarBody'. Render the result to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/rust_render.png and save the project to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/rust_bmw.blend.
